github_issue_workflow.overview: This workflow defines how to fetch and present GitHub issues assigned to the user. CRITICAL: Only show issues where the user is the assignee. Do not show other open issues or PRs.

step.title: Parse User Request
step.description: When user asks "What are the GitHub issues assigned to me in https://github.com/{org}/{repo}?"

actions.action: Extract the repository owner and name from the URL
Prepare to fetch ONLY issues assigned to the authenticated user

step.title: Use GitHub MCP to Fetch Assigned Issues
step.description: Use the list_issues tool with proper filtering

critical_parameters.parameter: The authenticated user (use their GitHub username)

step.title: Present ONLY Assigned Issues
step.description: Show only the issues returned from the filtered query

presentation_rules.rule: DO NOT fetch or show all open issues in the repository
DO NOT show PRs unless specifically requested
ONLY show issues where the user is the assignee
Format each issue clearly with number, title, and relevant details
Include CVE information if present in issue body

when_no_issues_found.format: No GitHub issues are currently assigned to you in {org}/{repo}.

anti_pattern.description: Showing all open issues in the repository
anti_pattern.why_wrong: User specifically asked for issues assigned to them
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Use assignee filter in list_issues call

anti_pattern.description: Including PRs in the response when only issues were requested
anti_pattern.why_wrong: PRs and issues are different; user asked for issues
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Only show issues unless PRs are explicitly requested

anti_pattern.description: Showing issues assigned to other team members
anti_pattern.why_wrong: User wants to see their own workload, not others'
anti_pattern.correct_approach: Filter by authenticated user's assignee status

case.name: user_not_authenticated
case.scenario: GitHub MCP cannot determine authenticated user
case.handling: Ask user for their GitHub username to use as assignee filter

case.name: multiple_assignees
case.scenario: Issue has multiple assignees including the user
case.handling: Include the issue since user is one of the assignees

case.name: repository_not_found
case.scenario: Repository URL is invalid or inaccessible
case.handling: Report error clearly and ask user to verify repository URL

validation_checklist.item: Confirmed repository owner and name from URL
Used assignee parameter in list_issues call
Presented ONLY issues assigned to user
Did NOT include issues assigned to others
Did NOT include PRs unless requested
